{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|千葉県|Q80011}}{{jalink|市原市|Q207881}}{{jalink|島野 (市原市)|Q122601140|島野}}1129・1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{jalink|シナツヒコ|Q1073925|志那都比古尊}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）第12代{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}40年 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|7月25日|Q2720}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''島穴神社'''（しまあなじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|千葉県|Q80011}}{{jalink|市原市|Q207881}}{{jalink|島野 (市原市)|Q122601140|島野}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の3柱。 ; 主祭神 :* '''{{jalink|シナツヒコ|Q1073925|志那都比古尊}}'''（しなつひこのみこと） - 風の神。社伝では、{{jalink|姉埼神社|Q11447235}}祭神の{{jalink|シナトベ||支那斗弁命}}の夫もしくは弟とする。 ; 配祀神 :* {{jalink|日本武尊}}（やまとたけるのみこと） :* {{jalink|倭姫命|Q4388732|倭比売尊}}（やまとひめのみこと） === 歴史 === === 創建 === 『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』・『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』では、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}（第12代）40年の{{jalink|日本武尊}}が東征の際、走水の海（{{jalink|浦賀水道|Q723431}}）で暴風雨に遭ったが妃の{{jalink|弟橘姫}}の犠牲によって上総に上陸することができたとする記事が見える。社伝ではその時のこととして、弟橘姫が{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の{{jalink|龍田大社|Q246455}}の神に、無事に上総まで航行させてくれるのなら風鎮めの神を祀ると祈ったので、日本武尊は弟橘姫命の遺志に従いこの地に志那都比古尊を祀ったとする。 また社伝では、日本武尊の死後の景行天皇57年[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 島穴神社]（神社のひろば）。、日本武尊の父の景行天皇は日本武尊東征の縁の地を歴訪し、当社に日本武尊命・倭比売尊の神霊を合祀したとする。 === 概史 === {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}元年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）に「島穴神」に正五位下勲五等の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が授けられた旨が記載されている。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|上総国|Q1055394}}{{jalink|海上郡 (上総国)|Q11558726|海上郡}}に「島穴神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542|和名抄}}』に見える地名のうちでは、当地は「海上郡島穴郷」に比定される{{Sfn|姉崎神社・島穴神社（神々）|1984}}。 かつては同じく式内社の{{jalink|姉埼神社|Q11447235}}と深い関係にあり、神輿が両社を行き来していたという。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列し、明治12年（{{jalink|1879年|Q7783}}）に{{jalink|県社}}に昇格した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}元年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）5月17日、従五位上勲五等から正五位下勲五等 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「島穴神」。 * 元慶8年（{{jalink|884年|Q23895}}）7月15日、正五位下勲五等から正五位上勲五等 （『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「島穴神」。 === 境内 === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|本殿 ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|神饌田
{{small|境内東側に所在。毎年5月にお田植祭、9月に抜穂祭が行われる。}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|宮之橋
{{small|大正8年造営。}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|鳥居 === 摂末社 === * 疱瘡神社 * 第六天神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|Q11152535|面足尊}}、{{jalink|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|Q11152535|惶根尊}} * 道祖神社 * 八雲神社 * 浅間神社 - {{jalink|富士塚|Q5507555}}の上に鎮座する。 境内にはほか数社がある。 === 関係地 === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|旧社地}}{{small|{{jalink|内房線|Q1142964}}を挟んで奥に現社地。}}]] * 旧社地 *: 現社地から北方へ約200メートル、線路を挟んだ地に所在する。島穴神社の旧鎮座地と伝わり、昔はかなりの広さを持っていたという。かつては林の中に深い穴があり、そこから常に清い風が常に吹いていることから風の神である志那都比古尊のなすところとされ、これに「島穴」の社名も因んだという{{Sfn|姉崎神社・島穴神社（神々）|1984}}。現在では穴は確認されておらず伝承の真偽は明らかでないが、伝承が古墳の石室を指す可能性が指摘されている{{Sfn|姉崎神社・島穴神社（神々）|1984}}。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=島穴神社|title={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103}}|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）542-543コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=島穴神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 12 千葉県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|島穴神社（平凡社）|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}|author=鈴木仲秋|year=1984|chapter=姉崎神社・島穴神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 11 関東|publisher={{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|姉崎神社・島穴神社（神々）|1984}}}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|姉埼神社|Q11447235}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 島穴神社] - 千葉県神社庁監修「神社のひろば」 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しまあなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:富士塚]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|千葉県|Q80011}}{{jalink|市原市|Q207881}}{{jalink|島野 (市原市)|Q122601140|島野}}1129・1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{jalink|シナツヒコ|Q1073925|志那都比古尊}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）第12代{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}40年 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|7月25日|Q2720}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''島穴神社'''（しまあなじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|千葉県|Q80011}}{{jalink|市原市|Q207881}}{{jalink|島野 (市原市)|Q122601140|島野}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の3柱。 ; 主祭神 :* '''{{jalink|シナツヒコ|Q1073925|志那都比古尊}}'''（しなつひこのみこと） - 風の神。社伝では、{{jalink|姉埼神社|Q11447235}}祭神の{{jalink|シナトベ||支那斗弁命}}の夫もしくは弟とする。 ; 配祀神 :* {{jalink|日本武尊}}（やまとたけるのみこと） :* {{jalink|倭姫命|Q4388732|倭比売尊}}（やまとひめのみこと） === 歴史 === === 創建 === 『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』・『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』では、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}（第12代）40年の{{jalink|日本武尊}}が東征の際、走水の海（{{jalink|浦賀水道|Q723431}}）で暴風雨に遭ったが妃の{{jalink|弟橘姫}}の犠牲によって上総に上陸することができたとする記事が見える。社伝ではその時のこととして、弟橘姫が{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の{{jalink|龍田大社|Q246455}}の神に、無事に上総まで航行させてくれるのなら風鎮めの神を祀ると祈ったので、日本武尊は弟橘姫命の遺志に従いこの地に志那都比古尊を祀ったとする。 また社伝では、日本武尊の死後の景行天皇57年[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 島穴神社]（神社のひろば）。、日本武尊の父の景行天皇は日本武尊東征の縁の地を歴訪し、当社に日本武尊命・倭比売尊の神霊を合祀したとする。 === 概史 === {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}元年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）に「島穴神」に正五位下勲五等の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が授けられた旨が記載されている。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|上総国|Q1055394}}{{jalink|海上郡 (上総国)|Q11558726|海上郡}}に「島穴神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542|和名抄}}』に見える地名のうちでは、当地は「海上郡島穴郷」に比定される{{Sfn|姉崎神社・島穴神社（神々）|1984}}。 かつては同じく式内社の{{jalink|姉埼神社|Q11447235}}と深い関係にあり、神輿が両社を行き来していたという。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列し、明治12年（{{jalink|1879年|Q7783}}）に{{jalink|県社}}に昇格した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}元年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）5月17日、従五位上勲五等から正五位下勲五等 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「島穴神」。 * 元慶8年（{{jalink|884年|Q23895}}）7月15日、正五位下勲五等から正五位上勲五等 （『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「島穴神」。 === 境内 === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|本殿 ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|神饌田
{{small|境内東側に所在。毎年5月にお田植祭、9月に抜穂祭が行われる。}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|宮之橋
{{small|大正8年造営。}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|鳥居 === 摂末社 === * 疱瘡神社 * 第六天神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|Q11152535|面足尊}}、{{jalink|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|Q11152535|惶根尊}} * 道祖神社 * 八雲神社 * 浅間神社 - {{jalink|富士塚|Q5507555}}の上に鎮座する。 境内にはほか数社がある。 === 関係地 === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|旧社地}}{{small|{{jalink|内房線|Q1142964}}を挟んで奥に現社地。}}]] * 旧社地 *: 現社地から北方へ約200メートル、線路を挟んだ地に所在する。島穴神社の旧鎮座地と伝わり、昔はかなりの広さを持っていたという。かつては林の中に深い穴があり、そこから常に清い風が常に吹いていることから風の神である志那都比古尊のなすところとされ、これに「島穴」の社名も因んだという{{Sfn|姉崎神社・島穴神社（神々）|1984}}。現在では穴は確認されておらず伝承の真偽は明らかでないが、伝承が古墳の石室を指す可能性が指摘されている{{Sfn|姉崎神社・島穴神社（神々）|1984}}。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=島穴神社|title={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103}}|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）542-543コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=島穴神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 12 千葉県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|島穴神社（平凡社）|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}|author=鈴木仲秋|year=1984|chapter=姉崎神社・島穴神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 11 関東|publisher={{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|姉崎神社・島穴神社（神々）|1984}}}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|姉埼神社|Q11447235}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 島穴神社] - 千葉県神社庁監修「神社のひろば」 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しまあなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:富士塚]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is located in [[Kazusa Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická 290-0034https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4040005009327 == Commons category (P373) == * Shimaana-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県市原市島野1129・1130', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shimaana-jinja}} [[JA:島穴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11477020}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Shimaana Shrine |画像 = [[File:Shimaana-jinja, haiden.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Ichihara|Q207881}}{{jalink|Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|Shimano}} 1129/1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|July 25|Q2720}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|Shimano}}, {{jalink|Ichihara|Q207881}}, {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{jalink|Shinatobe||Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}}. ; Associated deities :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{jalink|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{jalink|Uraga Channel|Q723431}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{jalink|Ototachibana-hime}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{jalink|Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455}} in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{jalink|court rank for deities|Q11591025}}). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}" compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it appears in {{jalink|Kazusa Province|Q1055394}}, {{jalink|Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|Unakami District}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{jalink|Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|Wamyōshō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}) it was listed as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{jalink|1879|Q7783}}) it was promoted to {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{jalink|884|Q23895}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{jalink|Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|Omotaru-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{jalink|fujizuka|Q5507555}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{jalink|Uchibō Line|Q1142964}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しまあなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:富士塚]] [[Category:need translation]]

== * {'text': '千葉県市原市島野1129・1130', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shimaana-jinja}} [[JA:島穴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11477020}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Shimaana Shrine |画像 = [[File:Shimaana-jinja, haiden.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Ichihara|Q207881}}{{jalink|Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|Shimano}} 1129/1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|July 25|Q2720}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|Shimano}}, {{jalink|Ichihara|Q207881}}, {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{jalink|Shinatobe||Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}}. ; Associated deities :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{jalink|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{jalink|Uraga Channel|Q723431}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{jalink|Ototachibana-hime}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{jalink|Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455}} in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{jalink|court rank for deities|Q11591025}}). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}" compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it appears in {{jalink|Kazusa Province|Q1055394}}, {{jalink|Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|Unakami District}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{jalink|Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|Wamyōshō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}) it was listed as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{jalink|1879|Q7783}}) it was promoted to {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{jalink|884|Q23895}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{jalink|Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|Omotaru-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{jalink|fujizuka|Q5507555}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{jalink|Uchibō Line|Q1142964}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しまあなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:富士塚]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{jalink|Shinatobe||Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}}. ; Associated deities :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{jalink|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{jalink|Uraga Channel|Q723431}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{jalink|Ototachibana-hime}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{jalink|Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455}} in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{jalink|court rank for deities|Q11591025}}). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}" compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it appears in {{jalink|Kazusa Province|Q1055394}}, {{jalink|Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|Unakami District}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{jalink|Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|Wamyōshō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}) it was listed as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{jalink|1879|Q7783}}) it was promoted to {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{jalink|884|Q23895}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{jalink|Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|Omotaru-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{jalink|fujizuka|Q5507555}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{jalink|Uchibō Line|Q1142964}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しまあなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:富士塚]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Ichihara|Q207881}}{{jalink|Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|Shimano}} 1129/1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|July 25|Q2720}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|Shimano}}, {{jalink|Ichihara|Q207881}}, {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{jalink|Shinatobe||Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}}. ; Associated deities :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{jalink|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{jalink|Uraga Channel|Q723431}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{jalink|Ototachibana-hime}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{jalink|Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455}} in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{jalink|court rank for deities|Q11591025}}). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}" compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it appears in {{jalink|Kazusa Province|Q1055394}}, {{jalink|Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|Unakami District}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{jalink|Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|Wamyōshō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}) it was listed as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{jalink|1879|Q7783}}) it was promoted to {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{jalink|884|Q23895}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{jalink|Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|Omotaru-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{jalink|fujizuka|Q5507555}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{jalink|Uchibō Line|Q1142964}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - == * {'text': '千葉県市原市島野1129・1130', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shimaana-jinja}} [[JA:島穴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11477020}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Shimaana Shrine |画像 = [[File:Shimaana-jinja, haiden.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Ichihara|Q207881}}{{jalink|Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|Shimano}} 1129/1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|July 25|Q2720}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|Shimano}}, {{jalink|Ichihara|Q207881}}, {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{jalink|Shinatobe||Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}}. ; Associated deities :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{jalink|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'' and ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{jalink|Uraga Channel|Q723431}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{jalink|Ototachibana-hime}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{jalink|Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455}} in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{jalink|court rank for deities|Q11591025}}). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}" compiled in {{jalink|Encho (era)|Q1327274|Encho}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), it appears in {{jalink|Kazusa Province|Q1055394}}, {{jalink|Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|Unakami District}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{jalink|Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|Wamyōshō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}) it was listed as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{jalink|1879|Q7783}}) it was promoted to {{jalink|kensha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{jalink|884|Q23895}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{jalink|Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|Omotaru-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{jalink|fujizuka|Q5507555}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{jalink|Uchibō Line|Q1142964}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{jalink|Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しまあなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:富士塚]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano}}, {{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{ill|lt=Shinatobe||WD=|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235}}. ; Associated deities :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{ill|lt=Uraga Channel|Q723431|WD=Q723431}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{ill||lt=Ototachibana-hime|UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}}" compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394}}, {{ill|lt=Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|WD=Q11558726|lt=Unakami District}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyōshō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}) it was listed as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}) it was promoted to {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Omotaru-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{ill|lt=fujizuka|Q5507555|WD=Q5507555}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しまあなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:富士塚]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しまあなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:富士塚]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[File:Shimaana-jinja, haiden.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881}}{{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano}} 1129/1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano}}, {{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{ill|lt=Shinatobe||WD=|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235}}. ; Associated deities :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{ill|lt=Uraga Channel|Q723431|WD=Q723431}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ototachibana-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}}" compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394}}, {{ill|lt=Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|WD=Q11558726|lt=Unakami District}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyōshō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}) it was listed as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Omotaru-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{ill|lt=fujizuka|Q5507555|WD=Q5507555}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しまあなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:富士塚]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
