former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}}, {{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{ill|lt=Shinatobe|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}. ; Associated deities :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{ill|lt=Uraga Channel|Q723431|WD=Q723431|1=Uraga Channel}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ototachibana-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|1=Tatsuta Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, {{ill|lt=Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|WD=Q11558726|lt=Unakami District|1=Unakami district}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyōshō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was listed as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Omotaru-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}, {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{ill|lt=fujizuka|Q5507555|WD=Q5507555|1=Fujizuka}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964|1=Uchibō Line}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:富士塚]][[Category:qq]]

Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, {{ill|lt=Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|WD=Q11558726|lt=Unakami District|1=Unakami district}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyōshō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was listed as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Omotaru-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}, {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{ill|lt=fujizuka|Q5507555|WD=Q5507555|1=Fujizuka}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964|1=Uchibō Line}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:富士塚]][[Category:qq]]

Shimaana Shrine |画像 = [[File:Shimaana-jinja, haiden.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}{{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}} 1129/1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}}, {{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{ill|lt=Shinatobe|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}. ; Associated deities :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{ill|lt=Uraga Channel|Q723431|WD=Q723431|1=Uraga Channel}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ototachibana-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|1=Tatsuta Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, {{ill|lt=Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|WD=Q11558726|lt=Unakami District|1=Unakami district}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyōshō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was listed as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Omotaru-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}, {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{ill|lt=fujizuka|Q5507555|WD=Q5507555|1=Fujizuka}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964|1=Uchibō Line}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - Shimaana Shrine |画像 = [[File:Shimaana-jinja, haiden.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}{{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}} 1129/1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}}, {{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{ill|lt=Shinatobe|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}. ; Associated deities :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{ill|lt=Uraga Channel|Q723431|WD=Q723431|1=Uraga Channel}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ototachibana-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|1=Tatsuta Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, {{ill|lt=Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|WD=Q11558726|lt=Unakami District|1=Unakami district}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyōshō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was listed as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Omotaru-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}, {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{ill|lt=fujizuka|Q5507555|WD=Q5507555|1=Fujizuka}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964|1=Uchibō Line}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:富士塚]][[Category:qq]]

Shimaana Shrine |画像 = [[File:Shimaana-jinja, haiden.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}{{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}} 1129/1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}}, {{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{ill|lt=Shinatobe|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}. ; Associated deities :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{ill|lt=Uraga Channel|Q723431|WD=Q723431|1=Uraga Channel}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ototachibana-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|1=Tatsuta Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, {{ill|lt=Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|WD=Q11558726|lt=Unakami District|1=Unakami district}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyōshō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was listed as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Omotaru-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}, {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{ill|lt=fujizuka|Q5507555|WD=Q5507555|1=Fujizuka}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964|1=Uchibō Line}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:市原市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Fujizuka]][[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}{{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}} 1129/1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}}, {{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{ill|lt=Shinatobe|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}. ; Associated deities :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{ill|lt=Uraga Channel|Q723431|WD=Q723431|1=Uraga Channel}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ototachibana-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|1=Tatsuta Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, {{ill|lt=Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|WD=Q11558726|lt=Unakami District|1=Unakami district}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyōshō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was listed as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Omotaru-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}, {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{ill|lt=fujizuka|Q5507555|WD=Q5507555|1=Fujizuka}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964|1=Uchibō Line}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Ichihara, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Fujizuka]][[Category:qq]]

Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, {{ill|lt=Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|WD=Q11558726|lt=Unakami District|1=Unakami district}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyōshō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was listed as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Omotaru-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}, {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{ill|lt=fujizuka|Q5507555|WD=Q5507555|1=Fujizuka}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964|1=Uchibō Line}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] - "Jinja no Hiroba," supervised by the Chiba Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimaana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Ichihara, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Fujizuka]]

Shimaana Shrine |画像 = [[File:Shimaana-jinja, haiden.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}{{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}} 1129/1130 |ISO = JP-12 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 40.15 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 4.44 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (12th emperor) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Chiba }} [[File:Shimaana-jinja, torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shimaana Shrine''' (しまあなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimano (Ichihara)|Q122601140|WD=Q122601140|lt=Shimano|1=Shimano}}, {{ill|lt=Ichihara|Q207881|WD=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}''' (しなつひこのみこと) - a wind deity. In shrine tradition, this deity is regarded as the husband or younger brother of {{ill|lt=Shinatobe|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}, the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}. ; Associated deities :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} (やまとひめのみこと) === History === === Founding === In the accounts of the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th emperor)'s 40th year includes the story that {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q1132476|WD=Q1132476|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Kuma District, Kumamoto}} encountered a violent storm at Hashirimizu Sea (the {{ill|lt=Uraga Channel|Q723431|WD=Q723431|1=Uraga Channel}}) during his eastern expedition, but was able to land safely in Kazusa through the self-sacrifice of his consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ototachibana-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says that at that time Ototachibana-hime prayed to the deity of {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|1=Tatsuta Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, vowing to enshrine a wind-calming god if safe passage to Kazusa were granted; in accordance with her last wish, Yamato Takeru enshrined Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto here. Shrine tradition also states that in the 57th year of Emperor Keiko, after Yamato Takeru's death,[http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba). Emperor Keiko, father of Yamato Takeru, visited places associated with Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign and jointly enshrined the divine spirits of Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto and Yamatohime-no-Mikoto at this shrine. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Shimaana-no-kami" was granted the rank of junior fifth rank, lower grade, merit grade five ({{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" compiled in {{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, {{ill|lt=Unakami District (Kazusa)|Q11558726|WD=Q11558726|lt=Unakami District|1=Unakami district}} as "Shimaana Jinja," and is listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Among place names in the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyōshō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', this area is identified with "Shimaano-go, Unakami District"{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. The shrine once had close ties with the likewise Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}}, and it is said the sacred palanquin traveled between the two shrines. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was listed as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Ranks granted to the deity === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), 5th month 17th day: promoted from junior fifth rank upper, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". * Gangyo 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), 7th month 15th day: promoted from senior fifth rank lower, merit grade five, to senior fifth rank upper, merit grade five (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Shimaana-no-kami". === Precinct === File:Shimaana-jinja, honden.JPG|Honden (main hall) ファイル:島穴神社 神饌田.JPG|Shinsen-den (sacred rice field)
{{small|Located on the east side of the precinct. A rice-planting festival is held every May, and a harvest festival every September.}} File:Shimaana-jinja, Miya-no-hashi.JPG|Miya-no-hashi Bridge
{{small|Built in Taisho 8 (1919).}} File:Shimaana-jinja, entrance.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Sorei Shrine * Dairokuten Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Omotaru-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}, {{ill|lt=Omotaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|WD=Q11152535|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}} * Dōsojin Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * Asama Shrine - enshrined atop a {{ill|lt=fujizuka|Q5507555|WD=Q5507555|1=Fujizuka}} mound. There are several other small shrines in the precinct. === Related site === [[File:Shimaana-jinja, former place.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine site}}{{small|The current shrine site is farther in beyond the {{ill|lt=Uchibō Line|Q1142964|WD=Q1142964|1=Uchibō Line}} tracks.}}]] * Former shrine site *: Located about 200 meters north of the present site, on the far side of the railway line. It is traditionally transmitted as the former seat of Shimaana Shrine, and is said to have once covered a considerable area. Formerly there was a deep cave in the woods, and because a clear wind was said to always blow from it, it was considered an action place of Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the wind deity; the shrine name "Shimaana" is also said to derive from this{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. Although no cave is confirmed today and the tradition cannot be verified, it has been suggested that the tradition may refer to the stone chamber of an ancient tumulus{{Sfn|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|author=|year=1912|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Jinja Shiryo|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/542}} ''Meiji Jinja Shiryo Fuken Gosha Vol. 1''] (National Diet Library Digital Collections), see frames 542-543. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Shimaana Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490121|ref={{Harvid|Shimaana Shrine (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Nakaaki Suzuki|year=1984|chapter=Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 11 Kanto|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|Anesaki Shrine and Shimaana Shrine (Kami-gami)|1984}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Anesaki Shrine|Q11447235|WD=Q11447235|1=Anesaki Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shimaana-jinja}} * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=2052 Shimaana Shrine] -
